revision.comment: 山口県の神社（うむむ、資料がない）
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=山口県護国神社| 所在地=山口県山口市宮野下1932| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|35|29|N|135|44|54|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=護国の[[英霊]]| 社格=内務大臣指定護国神社・別表神社|<!-- 創建=[[]]年（[[年]]）|--> 例祭=[[4月29日]]・[[11月3日]]}}
'''山口県護国神社'''（やまぐちけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[山口県]][[山口市]]にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）である。
山口県関係の[[明治維新]]以降の国難に殉じた護国の[[英霊]]を祀る。第二次大戦後は、山口県出身の殉職[[自衛官]]も合祀している。
==関連項目== *[[自衛官護国神社合祀事件]]
[[Category:山口県の神社|やまくちけんここくしんしや]] [[Category:護国神社|やまくちけんここくしんしや]] [[Category:別表神社|やまくちけんここくしんしや]]

revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=山口県護国神社| 所在地=山口県山口市宮野下1932| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|11|37|N|131|29|25|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=護国の[[英霊]]| 社格=内務大臣指定護国神社・別表神社|<!-- 創建=[[]]年（[[年]]）|--> 例祭=[[4月29日]]・[[11月3日]]}}
'''山口県護国神社'''（やまぐちけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[山口県]][[山口市]]にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）である。
山口県関係の[[明治維新]]以降の国難に殉じた護国の[[英霊]]を祀る。第二次大戦後は、山口県出身の殉職[[自衛官]]も合祀している。
==関連項目== *[[自衛官護国神社合祀事件]]
[[Category:山口県の神社|やまくちけんここくしんしや]] [[Category:護国神社|やまくちけんここくしんしや]] [[Category:別表神社|やまくちけんここくしんしや]]

{{神社| 名称=山口県護国神社| 所在地=山口県山口市宮野下1932| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|11|37|N|131|29|25|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=山口県関係の護国の[[英霊]]| 社格=内務大臣指定護国神社・別表神社| 創建=昭和16年（[[1941年]]）| 本殿=一間社[[流造]]| 例祭=[[4月29日]]・[[11月3日]]}}
'''山口県護国神社'''（やまぐちけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[山口県]][[山口市]]にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）である。
==祭神== 山口県関係の[[明治維新]]以降の国難に殉じた護国の[[英霊]]を祀る。この中には、[[吉田松陰]]、[[久坂玄瑞]]、[[来島又兵衛]]、[[大村益次郎]]、[[高杉晋作]]、[[月性]]も含まれている。
第二次大戦後は、山口県出身の殉職[[自衛官]]も合祀している。
==歴史== 山口県では、明治36年に防長靖献会が設立され、現鎮座地の南に隣接する桜畠練兵場（現 [[陸上自衛隊]][[山口駐屯地]]訓練場）で招魂祭が斎行されていた。
昭和14年（[[1939年]]）、山口県にも[[護国神社]]を創建することとなり、昭和16年（[[1941年]]）8月、現在地に社殿が竣工した。内務大臣から指定護国神社の指定を受け、同年11月26日に[[靖国神社]]の祭神となっていた山口県出身の英霊7159柱を合祀、翌27日に鎮座祭が斎行された。
==関連項目== *[[自衛官護国神社合祀事件]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:やまくちけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:山口市]]

==祭神== 山口県関係の[[明治維新]]以降の国難に殉じた護国の[[英霊]]を祀る。この中には、[[吉田松陰]]、[[久坂玄瑞]]、[[来島又兵衛]]、[[大村益次郎]]、[[高杉晋作]]、[[月性]]も含まれている。

{{出典の明記|date=2011年1月}} {{神社 |名称=山口縣護國神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=山口市}} |所在地=[[山口県]][[山口市]]平野二丁目2番1号 |ISO = JP-35 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 11 | 緯度秒 = 37.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 131 |経度分 = 29 | 経度秒 = 25.8 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |祭神=山口県関係の国事殉難者 |社格=内務大臣指定護国神社・別表神社 |創建=[[1941年]]（昭和16年） |本殿=一間社[[流造]] |例祭=[[4月29日]]・[[11月3日]] |地図 = Japan Yamaguchi }} {{Mapplot|131.490517|34.193774|山口縣護國神社}} '''山口縣護國神社'''（やまぐちけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[山口県]][[山口市]]にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）である。
== 祭神 == 山口県関係の[[明治維新]]以降の国難に殉じた戦没者を祀る。この中には、[[吉田松陰]]、[[久坂玄瑞]]、[[来島又兵衛]]、[[大村益次郎]]、[[高杉晋作]]、[[月性]]も含まれている。
[[第二次大戦]]後は、山口県出身の殉職[[自衛官]]も合祀している。
== 歴史 == 山口県では、[[1903年]]（[[明治]]36年）に防長靖献会が設立され、現鎮座地の南に隣接する桜畠練兵場（現 [[陸上自衛隊]][[山口駐屯地]]訓練場）で招魂祭が斎行されていた。
[[1939年]]（[[昭和]]14年）、山口県にも[[護国神社]]を創建することとなり、[[1941年]]（昭和16年）8月、現在地に社殿が竣工した。内務大臣から指定護国神社の指定を受け、同年11月26日に[[靖国神社]]の祭神となっていた山口県出身の戦没者7159柱を合祀、翌27日に鎮座祭が斎行された。

[[1939年]]（[[昭和]]14年）、山口県にも[[護国神社]]を創建することとなり、[[1941年]]（昭和16年）8月、現在地に社殿が竣工した。内務大臣から指定護国神社の指定を受け、同年11月26日に[[靖国神社]]の祭神となっていた山口県出身の戦没者7159柱を合祀、翌27日に鎮座祭が斎行された。

{{出典の明記|date=2011年1月}} {{神社 |名称=山口縣護國神社 |画像=[[File:Yamaguchi_gokoku_shrine.jpg|270px]]
{{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|marker=place-of-worship}} |所在地=[[山口県]][[山口市]]平野二丁目2番1号 |ISO = JP-35 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 11 | 緯度秒 = 37.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 131 |経度分 = 29 | 経度秒 = 25.8 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |祭神=山口県関係の国事殉難者 |社格=内務大臣指定護国神社・別表神社 |創建=[[1941年]]（昭和16年） |本殿=一間社[[流造]] |例祭=[[4月29日]]・[[11月3日]] |地図 = Japan Yamaguchi#Japan }} '''山口縣護國神社'''（やまぐちけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[山口県]][[山口市]]にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）である。 == 祭神 == 山口県関係の[[明治維新]]以降の国難に殉じた戦没者を祀る。この中には、[[吉田松陰]]、[[久坂玄瑞]]、[[来島又兵衛]]、[[大村益次郎]]、[[高杉晋作]]、[[月性]]も含まれている。 [[第二次世界大戦]]後は、山口県出身の殉職[[自衛官]]も合祀している。 == 歴史 == 山口県では、[[1903年]]（[[明治]]36年）に防長靖献会が設立され、現鎮座地の南に隣接する桜畠練兵場（現 [[陸上自衛隊]][[山口駐屯地]]訓練場）で招魂祭が斎行されていた。 [[1939年]]（[[昭和]]14年）、山口県にも[[護国神社]]を創建することとなり、[[1941年]]（昭和16年）8月、現在地に社殿が竣工した。内務大臣から指定護国神社の指定を受け、同年11月26日に[[靖国神社]]の祭神となっていた山口県出身の戦没者7159柱を合祀、翌27日に鎮座祭が斎行された。 == 所在地情報 == ; 所在地 :* 山口県山口市平野二丁目2番1号 ; 交通 :* 鉄道 :** [[山口駅 (山口県)|山口駅]]（[[西日本旅客鉄道]]（JR西日本）[[山口線]]） - 車で10分 == 周辺 == * [[今八幡宮]] * [[常栄寺 (山口市)|常栄寺]] * [[豊榮神社・野田神社]] * [[山口市菜香亭]] * [[雲谷庵]]跡 * [[瑠璃光寺]] * [[洞春寺]]、のむら美術館 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''注釈''' {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} '''出典''' {{Reflist}} '''参考文献''' == 関連項目 == * [[国家神道]] * [[戦争祈念施設]] * [[自衛官護国神社合祀事件]] == 外部リンク == {{Osm box|n|4864182037}} * [http://yamaguchi-gokoku.jp/ 山口縣護國神社] {{新宗教}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまくちけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口市の建築物]] [[Category:山口市の歴史]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:別表神社]]

{{出典の明記|date=2011年1月}} {{神社 |名称=山口縣護國神社 |画像=[[File:Yamaguchi_gokoku_shrine.jpg|270px]]

revision.text:
{{Cite web|date=2011年1月}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine |image=[[File:Yamaguchi_gokoku_shrine.jpg|270px]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|marker=place-of-worship}} |location={{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}} {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市}} Hirano 2-2-1 |ISO=JP-35 |latitude degrees=34 |rank minutes=11 |rank seconds=37.5 |N/S=N |longitude degrees=131 |longitude minutes=29 |longitude seconds=25.8 |E/W=E |deity=National martyrs related to Yamaguchi Prefecture |rank={{ill|Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社#指定護国神社}}・{{ill|Special Shrines|ja|別表神社}} |founded=[[1941]] (Showa 16) |main Hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |festival=[[April 29]]・[[November 3]] |map=Japan Yamaguchi#Japan }} '''Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine'''（山口縣護國神社{{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|山口縣護國神社}}）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the war dead from Yamaguchi Prefecture who perished in national crises since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. This includes notable figures such as {{ill|Yoshida Shoin|ja|吉田松陰}}, {{ill|Kusaka Genzui|ja|久坂玄瑞}}, {{ill|Kijima Matabei|ja|来島又兵衛}}, {{ill|Omura Masujiro|ja|大村益次郎}}, {{ill|Takasugi Shinsaku|ja|高杉晋作}}, and {{ill|Gessho|ja|月性}}. After {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, Self-Defense Force personnel from Yamaguchi Prefecture who died in the line of duty have also been enshrined. == History == In 1903 (Meiji 36), the {{ill|Bocho Yasukenkai|ja|防長靖献会}} was established in Yamaguchi Prefecture, and memorial services were held at the Sakura Batake parade ground (now the training ground for the {{ill|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|ja|陸上自衛隊}} {{ill|Yamaguchi Garrison|ja|山口駐屯地}}) adjacent to the current location. In 1939 (Showa 14), it was decided to establish a Gokoku Shrine in Yamaguchi Prefecture. The shrine was completed at its current location in August 1941 (Showa 16). It was designated as an Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine, and on November 26 of the same year, 7159 spirits from Yamaguchi Prefecture who were enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} were transferred here. The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : {{ill|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|ja|山口駅}} ({{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} (JR West) {{ill|Yamaguchi Line|ja|山口線}}) - 10 minutes by car == Surroundings == * {{ill|Ima Hachiman Shrine|ja|今八幡宮}} * {{ill|Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|ja|常栄寺}} * {{ill|Toei Shrine and Noda Shrine|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}} * {{ill|Saikotei|ja|山口市菜香亭}} * {{ill|Kumotani-an|ja|雲谷庵}} ruins * {{ill|Rurikō-ji|ja|瑠璃光寺}} * {{ill|Dōshun-ji|ja|洞春寺}}, Nomura Art Museum == References == {{Reflist}} '''Notes''' {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} '''Citations''' {{Reflist}} '''References''' [[ja:山口縣護國神社]] == See also == * {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道}} * {{ill|War memorial|ja|戦争祈念施設}} * {{ill|Self-Defense Forces Gokoku Shrine Enshrinement Incident|ja|自衛官護国神社合祀事件}} == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4864182037}} * [http://yamaguchi-gokoku.jp/ Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Yamaguchi City Buildings]] [[Category:Yamaguchi City History]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]{{Gokoku Shrines}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]]

This includes notable figures such as {{ill|Yoshida Shoin|ja|吉田松陰}}, {{ill|Kusaka Genzui|ja|久坂玄瑞}}, {{ill|Kijima Matabei|ja|来島又兵衛}}, {{ill|Omura Masujiro|ja|大村益次郎}}, {{ill|Takasugi Shinsaku|ja|高杉晋作}}, and {{ill|Gessho|ja|月性}}. The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : {{ill|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|ja|山口駅}} ({{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} (JR West) {{ill|Yamaguchi Line|ja|山口線}}) - 10 minutes by car == Surroundings == * {{ill|Ima Hachiman Shrine|ja|今八幡宮}} * {{ill|Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|ja|常栄寺}} * {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}} * {{ill|Saikotei|ja|山口市菜香亭}} * {{ill|Kumotani-an|ja|雲谷庵}} ruins * {{ill|Rurikō-ji|ja|瑠璃光寺}} * {{ill|Dōshun-ji|ja|洞春寺}}, Nomura Art Museum == References == {{Reflist}} '''Notes''' {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} '''Citations''' {{Reflist}} '''References''' [[ja:山口縣護國神社]] == See also == * {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道}} * {{ill|War memorial|ja|戦争祈念施設}} * {{ill|Self-Defense Forces Gokoku Shrine Enshrinement Incident|ja|自衛官護国神社合祀事件}} == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4864182037}} * [http://yamaguchi-gokoku.jp/ Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Yamaguchi City Buildings]] [[Category:Yamaguchi City History]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]{{Gokoku Shrines}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]]

In 1939 (Showa 14), it was decided to establish a Gokoku Shrine in Yamaguchi Prefecture. The shrine was completed at its current location in August 1941 (Showa 16). It was designated as an Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine, and on November 26 of the same year, 7159 spirits from Yamaguchi Prefecture who were enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} were transferred here. The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : {{ill|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|ja|山口駅}} ({{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} (JR West) {{ill|Yamaguchi Line|ja|山口線}}) - 10 minutes by car == Surroundings == * {{ill|Ima Hachiman Shrine|ja|今八幡宮}} * After {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, Self-Defense Force personnel from Yamaguchi Prefecture who died in the line of duty have also been enshrined. == History == In 1903 (Meiji 36), the {{ill|Bocho Yasukenkai|ja|防長靖献会}} was established in Yamaguchi Prefecture, and memorial services were held at the Sakura Batake parade ground (now the training ground for the {{ill|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|ja|陸上自衛隊}} {{ill|Yamaguchi Garrison|ja|山口駐屯地}}) adjacent to the current location. In 1939 (Showa 14), it was decided to establish a Gokoku Shrine in Yamaguchi Prefecture. The shrine was completed at its current location in August 1941 (Showa 16). It was designated as an Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine, and on November 26 of the same year, 7159 spirits from Yamaguchi Prefecture who were enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} were transferred here. The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : {{ill|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|ja|山口駅}} ({{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} (JR West) {{ill|Yamaguchi Line|ja|山口線}}) - 10 minutes by car == Surroundings == * {{ill|Ima Hachiman Shrine|ja|今八幡宮}} * {{ill|Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|ja|常栄寺}} * {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}} * {{ill|Saikotei|ja|山口市菜香亭}} * {{ill|Kumotani-an|ja|雲谷庵}} ruins * {{ill|Rurikō-ji|ja|瑠璃光寺}} * {{ill|Dōshun-ji|ja|洞春寺}}, Nomura Art Museum == References == {{Reflist}} '''Notes''' {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} '''Citations''' {{Reflist}} '''References''' [[Category:dd]][[ja:山口縣護國神社]] == See also == * {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道}} * {{ill|War memorial|ja|戦争祈念施設}} * {{ill|Self-Defense Forces Gokoku Shrine Enshrinement Incident|ja|自衛官護国神社合祀事件}} == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4864182037}} * [http://yamaguchi-gokoku.jp/ Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Yamaguchi City Buildings]] [[Category:Yamaguchi City History]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]{{Gokoku Shrines}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:dardar]] [[Category:ファイル]]

This includes notable figures such as {{ill|Yoshida Shoin|ja|吉田松陰}}, {{ill|Kusaka Genzui|ja|久坂玄瑞}}, {{ill|Kijima Matabei|ja|来島又兵衛}}, {{ill|Omura Masujiro|ja|大村益次郎}}, {{ill|Takasugi Shinsaku|ja|高杉晋作}}, and {{ill|Gessho|ja|月性}}. After {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, Self-Defense Force personnel from Yamaguchi Prefecture who died in the line of duty have also been enshrined. == History == In 1903 (Meiji 36), the {{ill|Bocho Yasukenkai|ja|防長靖献会}} was established in Yamaguchi Prefecture, and memorial services were held at the Sakura Batake parade ground (now the training ground for the {{ill|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|ja|陸上自衛隊}} {{ill|Yamaguchi Garrison|ja|山口駐屯地}}) adjacent to the current location. In 1939 (Showa 14), it was decided to establish a Gokoku Shrine in Yamaguchi Prefecture. The shrine was completed at its current location in August 1941 (Showa 16). It was designated as an Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine, and on November 26 of the same year, 7159 spirits from Yamaguchi Prefecture who were enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} were transferred here. The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : {{ill|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|ja|山口駅}} ({{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} (JR West) {{ill|Yamaguchi Line|ja|山口線}}) - 10 minutes by car == Surroundings == * {{ill|Ima Hachiman Shrine|ja|今八幡宮}} * {{ill|Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|ja|常栄寺}} * {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}} * {{ill|Saikotei|ja|山口市菜香亭}} * {{ill|Kumotani-an|ja|雲谷庵}} ruins * {{ill|Rurikō-ji|ja|瑠璃光寺}} * {{ill|Dōshun-ji|ja|洞春寺}}, Nomura Art Museum == References == {{Reflist}} '''Notes''' {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} '''Citations''' {{Reflist}} '''References''' [[Category:dd]][[ja:山口縣護國神社]] == See also == * {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道}} * {{ill|War memorial|ja|戦争祈念施設}} * {{ill|Self-Defense Forces Gokoku Shrine Enshrinement Incident|ja|自衛官護国神社合祀事件}} == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4864182037}} * [http://yamaguchi-gokoku.jp/ Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Yamaguchi City Buildings]] [[Category:Yamaguchi City History]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]{{Gokoku Shrines}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]]

{{Cite web|date=2011年1月}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine |image=[[File:Yamaguchi_gokoku_shrine.jpg|270px]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|marker=place-of-worship}} |location={{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}} {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市}} Hirano 2-2-1 |ISO=JP-35 |latitude degrees=34 |rank minutes=11 |rank seconds=37.5 |N/S=N |longitude degrees=131 |longitude minutes=29 |longitude seconds=25.8 |E/W=E |deity=National martyrs related to Yamaguchi Prefecture |rank={{ill|Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社#指定護国神社}}・{{ill|Beppyo Shrines|ja|別表神社}} |founded=[[1941]] (Showa 16) |main Hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |festival=[[April 29]]・[[November 3]] |map=Japan Yamaguchi#Japan }} '''Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine'''（山口縣護國神社{{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|山口縣護國神社}}）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the war dead from Yamaguchi Prefecture who perished in national crises since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. This includes notable figures such as {{ill|Yoshida Shoin|ja|吉田松陰}}, {{ill|Kusaka Genzui|ja|久坂玄瑞}}, {{ill|Kijima Matabei|ja|来島又兵衛}}, {{ill|Omura Masujiro|ja|大村益次郎}}, {{ill|Takasugi Shinsaku|ja|高杉晋作}}, and {{ill|Gessho|ja|月性}}. After {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, Self-Defense Force personnel from Yamaguchi Prefecture who died in the line of duty have also been enshrined. == History == In 1903 (Meiji 36), the {{ill|Bocho Yasukenkai|ja|防長靖献会}} was established in Yamaguchi Prefecture, and memorial services were held at the Sakura Batake parade ground (now the training ground for the {{ill|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|ja|陸上自衛隊}} {{ill|Yamaguchi Garrison|ja|山口駐屯地}}) adjacent to the current location. In 1939 (Showa 14), it was decided to establish a Gokoku Shrine in Yamaguchi Prefecture. The shrine was completed at its current location in August 1941 (Showa 16). It was designated as an Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine, and on November 26 of the same year, 7159 spirits from Yamaguchi Prefecture who were enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} were transferred here. The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : {{ill|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|ja|山口駅}} ({{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} (JR West) {{ill|Yamaguchi Line|ja|山口線}}) - 10 minutes by car == Surroundings == * {{ill|Ima Hachiman Shrine|ja|今八幡宮}} * {{ill|Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|ja|常栄寺}} * {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}} * {{ill|Saikotei|ja|山口市菜香亭}} * {{ill|Kumotani-an|ja|雲谷庵}} ruins * {{ill|Rurikō-ji|ja|瑠璃光寺}} * {{ill|Dōshun-ji|ja|洞春寺}}, Nomura Art Museum == References == {{Reflist}} '''Notes''' {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} '''Citations''' {{Reflist}} '''References''' [[Category:dd]][[ja:山口縣護國神社]] == See also == * {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道}} * {{ill|War memorial|ja|戦争祈念施設}} * {{ill|Self-Defense Forces Gokoku Shrine Enshrinement Incident|ja|自衛官護国神社合祀事件}} == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4864182037}} * [http://yamaguchi-gokoku.jp/ Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Yamaguchi City Buildings]] [[Category:Yamaguchi City History]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]{{Gokoku Shrines}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTYamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTYamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTYamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]]

The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : {{ill|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|ja|山口駅}} ({{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} (JR West) {{ill|Yamaguchi Line|ja|山口線}}) - 10 minutes by car == Surroundings == * {{ill|Ima Hachiman Shrine|ja|今八幡宮}} * {{ill|Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|ja|常栄寺}} * {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}} * {{ill|Saikotei|ja|山口市菜香亭}} * {{ill|Kumotani-an|ja|雲谷庵}} ruins * {{ill|Rurikō-ji|ja|瑠璃光寺}} * {{ill|Dōshun-ji|ja|洞春寺}}, Nomura Art Museum == References == {{Reflist}} '''Notes''' {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} '''Citations''' {{Reflist}} '''References''' [[Category:dd]][[ja:山口縣護國神社]] == See also == * {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道}} * {{ill|War memorial|ja|戦争祈念施設}} * {{ill|Self-Defense Forces Gokoku Shrine Enshrinement Incident|ja|自衛官護国神社合祀事件}} == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4864182037}} * [http://yamaguchi-gokoku.jp/ Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{DEFAULTSORT::Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Yamaguchi City Buildings]] [[Category:Yamaguchi City History]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]{{Gokoku Shrines}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]]

This includes notable figures such as {{ill|Yoshida Shoin|ja|吉田松陰}}, {{ill|Kusaka Genzui|ja|久坂玄瑞}}, {{ill|Kijima Matabei|ja|来島又兵衛}}, {{ill|Omura Masujiro|ja|大村益次郎}}, {{ill|Takasugi Shinsaku|ja|高杉晋作}}, and {{ill|Gessho|ja|月性}}. After {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, Self-Defense Force personnel from Yamaguchi Prefecture who died in the line of duty have also been enshrined. == History == In 1903 (Meiji 36), the {{ill|Bocho Yasukenkai|ja|防長靖献会}} was established in Yamaguchi Prefecture, and memorial services were held at the Sakura Batake parade ground (now the training ground for the {{ill|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|ja|陸上自衛隊}} {{ill|Yamaguchi Garrison|ja|山口駐屯地}}) adjacent to the current location. It was designated as an Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine, and on November 26 of the same year, 7159 spirits from Yamaguchi Prefecture who were enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} were transferred here. This includes notable figures such as {{ill|Yoshida Shoin|ja|吉田松陰}}, {{ill|Kusaka Genzui|ja|久坂玄瑞}}, {{ill|Kijima Matabei|ja|来島又兵衛}}, {{ill|Omura Masujiro|ja|大村益次郎}}, {{ill|Takasugi Shinsaku|ja|高杉晋作}}, and {{ill|Gessho|ja|月性}}. After {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, Self-Defense Force personnel from Yamaguchi Prefecture who died in the line of duty have also been enshrined. == History == In 1903 (Meiji 36), the {{ill|Bocho Yasukenkai|ja|防長靖献会}} was established in Yamaguchi Prefecture, and memorial services were held at the Sakura Batake parade ground (now the training ground for the {{ill|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|ja|陸上自衛隊}} {{ill|Yamaguchi Garrison|ja|山口駐屯地}}) adjacent to the current location. In 1939 (Showa 14), it was decided to establish a Gokoku Shrine in Yamaguchi Prefecture. The shrine was completed at its current location in August 1941 (Showa 16). It was designated as an Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine, and on November 26 of the same year, 7159 spirits from Yamaguchi Prefecture who were enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} were transferred here. The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : {{ill|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|ja|山口駅}} ({{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} (JR West) {{ill|Yamaguchi Line|ja|山口線}}) - 10 minutes by car == Surroundings == * {{ill|Ima Hachiman Shrine|ja|今八幡宮}} * {{ill|Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|ja|常栄寺}} * {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}} * {{ill|Saikotei|ja|山口市菜香亭}} * {{ill|Kumotani-an|ja|雲谷庵}} ruins * {{ill|Rurikō-ji|ja|瑠璃光寺}} * {{ill|Dōshun-ji|ja|洞春寺}}, Nomura Art Museum == References == {{Reflist}} '''Notes''' {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} '''Citations''' {{Reflist}} '''References''' [[Category:dd]][[ja:山口縣護國神社]] == See also == * {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道}} * {{ill|War memorial|ja|戦争祈念施設}} * {{ill|Self-Defense Forces Gokoku Shrine Enshrinement Incident|ja|自衛官護国神社合祀事件}} == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4864182037}} * [http://yamaguchi-gokoku.jp/ Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{citation needed|Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Yamaguchi City Buildings]] [[Category:Yamaguchi City History]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]{{Gokoku Shrines}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]]

{{Cite web|date=2011年1月}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine |image=[[File:Yamaguchi_gokoku_shrine.jpg|270px]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|marker=place-of-worship}} |location={{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}} {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市}} Hirano 2-2-1 |ISO=JP-35 |latitude degrees=34 |rank minutes=11 |rank seconds=37.5 |N/S=N |longitude degrees=131 |longitude minutes=29 |longitude seconds=25.8 |E/W=E |deity=National martyrs related to Yamaguchi Prefecture |rank={{ill|Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社#指定護国神社}}・{{ill|Beppyo Shrines|ja|別表神社}} |founded=[[1941]] (Showa 16) |main Hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |festival=[[April 29]]・[[November 3]] |map=Japan Yamaguchi#Japan }} '''Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine'''（山口縣護國神社{{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|山口縣護國神社}}）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the war dead from Yamaguchi Prefecture who perished in national crises since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. This includes notable figures such as {{ill|Yoshida Shoin|ja|吉田松陰}}, {{ill|Kusaka Genzui|ja|久坂玄瑞}}, {{ill|Kijima Matabei|ja|来島又兵衛}}, {{ill|Omura Masujiro|ja|大村益次郎}}, {{ill|Takasugi Shinsaku|ja|高杉晋作}}, and {{ill|Gessho|ja|月性}}. After {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, Self-Defense Force personnel from Yamaguchi Prefecture who died in the line of duty have also been enshrined. == History == In 1903 (Meiji 36), the {{ill|Bocho Yasukenkai|ja|防長靖献会}} was established in Yamaguchi Prefecture, and memorial services were held at the Sakura Batake parade ground (now the training ground for the {{ill|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|ja|陸上自衛隊}} {{ill|Yamaguchi Garrison|ja|山口駐屯地}}) adjacent to the current location. In 1939 (Showa 14), it was decided to establish a Gokoku Shrine in Yamaguchi Prefecture. The shrine was completed at its current location in August 1941 (Showa 16). It was designated as an Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine, and on November 26 of the same year, 7159 spirits from Yamaguchi Prefecture who were enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} were transferred here. The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : {{ill|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|ja|山口駅}} ({{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} (JR West) {{ill|Yamaguchi Line|ja|山口線}}) - {{Cite web|date=2011年1月}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine |image=[[File:Yamaguchi_gokoku_shrine.jpg|270px]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|marker=place-of-worship}} |location={{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}} {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市}} Hirano 2-2-1 |ISO=JP-35 |latitude degrees=34 |rank minutes=11 |rank seconds=37.5 |N/S=N |longitude degrees=131 |longitude minutes=29 |longitude seconds=25.8 |E/W=E |deity=National martyrs related to Yamaguchi Prefecture |rank={{ill|Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社#指定護国神社}}・{{ill|Beppyo Shrines|ja|別表神社}} |founded=[[1941]] (Showa 16) |main Hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |festival=[[April 29]]・[[November 3]] |map=Japan Yamaguchi#Japan }} '''Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine'''（山口縣護國神社{{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|山口縣護國神社}}）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the war dead from Yamaguchi Prefecture who perished in national crises since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. This includes notable figures such as {{ill|Yoshida Shoin|ja|吉田松陰}}, {{ill|Kusaka Genzui|ja|久坂玄瑞}}, {{ill|Kijima Matabei|ja|来島又兵衛}}, {{ill|Omura Masujiro|ja|大村益次郎}}, {{ill|Takasugi Shinsaku|ja|高杉晋作}}, and {{ill|Gessho|ja|月性}}. After {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, Self-Defense Force personnel from Yamaguchi Prefecture who died in the line of duty have also been enshrined. == History == In 1903 (Meiji 36), the {{ill|Bocho Yasukenkai|ja|防長靖献会}} was established in Yamaguchi Prefecture, and memorial services were held at the Sakura Batake parade ground (now the training ground for the {{ill|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|ja|陸上自衛隊}} {{ill|Yamaguchi Garrison|ja|山口駐屯地}}) adjacent to the current location. In 1939 (Showa 14), it was decided to establish a Gokoku Shrine in Yamaguchi Prefecture. The shrine was completed at its current location in August 1941 (Showa 16). It was designated as an Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine, and on November 26 of the same year, 7159 spirits from Yamaguchi Prefecture who were enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} were transferred here. The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : {{ill|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|ja|山口駅}} ({{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} (JR West) {{ill|Yamaguchi Line|ja|山口線}}) - 10 minutes by car == Surroundings == * {{ill|Ima Hachiman Shrine|ja|今八幡宮}} * {{ill|Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|ja|常栄寺}} * {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}} * {{ill|Saikotei|ja|山口市菜香亭}} * {{ill|Kumotani-an|ja|雲谷庵}} ruins * {{ill|Rurikō-ji|ja|瑠璃光寺}} * {{ill|Dōshun-ji|ja|洞春寺}}, Nomura Art Museum == References == {{Reflist}} '''Notes''' {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} '''Citations''' {{Reflist}} '''References''' [[Category:dd]][[ja:山口縣護國神社]] == See also == * {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道}} * {{ill|War memorial|ja|戦争祈念施設}} * {{ill|Self-Defense Forces Gokoku Shrine Enshrinement Incident|ja|自衛官護国神社合祀事件}} == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4864182037}} * [http://yamaguchi-gokoku.jp/ Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{citation needed|Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Yamaguchi City Buildings]] [[Category:Yamaguchi City History]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:bump]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:qq\]] {{Beppyo Shrines}}

It was designated as an Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine, and on November 26 of the same year, 7159 spirits from Yamaguchi Prefecture who were enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} were transferred here. {{Cite web|date=2011年1月}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine |image=[[File:Yamaguchi_gokoku_shrine.jpg|270px]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|marker=place-of-worship}} |location={{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}} {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市}} Hirano 2-2-1 |ISO=JP-35 |latitude degrees=34 |rank minutes=11 |rank seconds=37.5 |N/S=N |longitude degrees=131 |longitude minutes=29 |longitude seconds=25.8 |E/W=E |deity=National martyrs related to Yamaguchi Prefecture |rank={{ill|Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社#指定護国神社}}・{{ill|Beppyo Shrines|ja|別表神社}} |founded=[[1941]] (Showa 16) |main Hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |festival=[[April 29]]・[[November 3]] |map=Japan Yamaguchi#Japan }} '''Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine'''（山口縣護國神社{{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|山口縣護國神社}}）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the war dead from Yamaguchi Prefecture who perished in national crises since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. This includes notable figures such as {{ill|Yoshida Shoin|ja|吉田松陰}}, {{ill|Kusaka Genzui|ja|久坂玄瑞}}, {{ill|Kijima Matabei|ja|来島又兵衛}}, {{ill|Omura Masujiro|ja|大村益次郎}}, {{ill|Takasugi Shinsaku|ja|高杉晋作}}, and {{ill|Gessho|ja|月性}}. After {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, Self-Defense Force personnel from Yamaguchi Prefecture who died in the line of duty have also been enshrined. == History == In 1903 (Meiji 36), the {{ill|Bocho Yasukenkai|ja|防長靖献会}} was established in Yamaguchi Prefecture, and memorial services were held at the Sakura Batake parade ground (now the training ground for the {{ill|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|ja|陸上自衛隊}} {{ill|Yamaguchi Garrison|ja|山口駐屯地}}) adjacent to the current location. In 1939 (Showa 14), it was decided to establish a Gokoku Shrine in Yamaguchi Prefecture. The shrine was completed at its current location in August 1941 (Showa 16). It was designated as an Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine, and on November 26 of the same year, 7159 spirits from Yamaguchi Prefecture who were enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} were transferred here. The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : {{ill|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|ja|山口駅}} ({{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} (JR West) {{ill|Yamaguchi Line|ja|山口線}}) - 10 minutes by car == Surroundings == * {{ill|Ima Hachiman Shrine|ja|今八幡宮}} * {{ill|Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|ja|常栄寺}} * {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}} * {{ill|Saikotei|ja|山口市菜香亭}} * {{ill|Kumotani-an|ja|雲谷庵}} ruins * {{ill|Rurikō-ji|ja|瑠璃光寺}} * {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|marker=place-of-worship}} |location={{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}} {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市}} Hirano 2-2-1 |ISO=JP-35 |latitude degrees=34 |rank minutes=11 |rank seconds=37.5 |N/S=N |longitude degrees=131 |longitude minutes=29 |longitude seconds=25.8 |E/W=E |deity=National martyrs related to Yamaguchi Prefecture |rank={{ill|Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社#指定護国神社}}・{{ill|Beppyo Shrines|ja|別表神社}} |founded=[[1941]] (Showa 16) |main Hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |festival=[[April 29]]・[[November 3]] |map=Japan Yamaguchi#Japan }} '''Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine'''（山口縣護國神社{{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|山口縣護國神社}}）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the war dead from Yamaguchi Prefecture who perished in national crises since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. This includes notable figures such as {{ill|Yoshida Shoin|ja|吉田松陰}}, {{ill|Kusaka Genzui|ja|久坂玄瑞}}, {{ill|Kijima Matabei|ja|来島又兵衛}}, {{ill|Omura Masujiro|ja|大村益次郎}}, {{ill|Takasugi Shinsaku|ja|高杉晋作}}, and {{ill|Gessho|ja|月性}}. After {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, Self-Defense Force personnel from Yamaguchi Prefecture who died in the line of duty have also been enshrined. == History == In 1903 (Meiji 36), the {{ill|Bocho Yasukenkai|ja|防長靖献会}} was established in Yamaguchi Prefecture, and memorial services were held at the Sakura Batake parade ground (now the training ground for the {{ill|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|ja|陸上自衛隊}} {{ill|Yamaguchi Garrison|ja|山口駐屯地}}) adjacent to the current location. In 1939 (Showa 14), it was decided to establish a Gokoku Shrine in Yamaguchi Prefecture. The shrine was completed at its current location in August 1941 (Showa 16). It was designated as an Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine, and on November 26 of the same year, 7159 spirits from Yamaguchi Prefecture who were enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} were transferred here. The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : {{ill|Yamaguchi Station {{Cite web|date=2011年1月}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine |image=[[File:Yamaguchi_gokoku_shrine.jpg|270px]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|marker=place-of-worship}} |location={{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}} {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市}} Hirano 2-2-1 |ISO=JP-35 |latitude degrees=34 |rank minutes=11 |rank seconds=37.5 |N/S=N |longitude degrees=131 |longitude minutes=29 |longitude seconds=25.8 |E/W=E |deity=National martyrs related to Yamaguchi Prefecture |rank={{ill|Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社#指定護国神社}}・{{ill|Beppyo Shrines|ja|別表神社}} |founded=[[1941]] (Showa 16) |main Hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |festival=[[April 29]]・[[November 3]] |map=Japan Yamaguchi#Japan }} '''Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine'''（山口縣護國神社{{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|山口縣護國神社}}）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the war dead from Yamaguchi Prefecture who perished in national crises since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. This includes notable figures such as {{ill|Yoshida Shoin|ja|吉田松陰}}, {{ill|Kusaka Genzui|ja|久坂玄瑞}}, {{ill|Kijima Matabei|ja|来島又兵衛}}, {{ill|Omura Masujiro|ja|大村益次郎}}, {{ill|Takasugi Shinsaku|ja|高杉晋作}}, and {{ill|Gessho|ja|月性}}. After {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, Self-Defense Force personnel from Yamaguchi Prefecture who died in the line of duty have also been enshrined. == History == In 1903 (Meiji 36), the {{ill|Bocho Yasukenkai|ja|防長靖献会}} was established in Yamaguchi Prefecture, and memorial services were held at the Sakura Batake parade ground (now the training ground for the {{ill|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|ja|陸上自衛隊}} {{ill|Yamaguchi Garrison|ja|山口駐屯地}}) adjacent to the current location. In 1939 (Showa 14), it was decided to establish a Gokoku Shrine in Yamaguchi Prefecture. The shrine was completed at its current location in August 1941 (Showa 16). It was designated as an Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine, and on November 26 of the same year, 7159 spirits from Yamaguchi Prefecture who were enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} were transferred here. The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : {{ill|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|ja|山口駅}} ({{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} (JR West) {{ill|Yamaguchi Line|ja|山口線}}) - 10 minutes by car == Surroundings == * {{ill|Ima Hachiman Shrine|ja|今八幡宮}} * {{ill|Dōshun-ji|ja|洞春寺}}, Nomura Art Museum == References == {{Reflist}} '''Notes''' {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} '''Citations''' {{Reflist}} '''References''' [[Category:dd]][[ja:山口縣護國神社]] == See also == {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|marker=place-of-worship}} |location={{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}} {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市}} Hirano 2-2-1 |ISO=JP-35 |latitude degrees=34 |rank minutes=11 |rank seconds=37.5 |N/S=N |longitude degrees=131 |longitude minutes=29 |longitude seconds=25.8 |E/W=E |deity=National martyrs related to Yamaguchi Prefecture |rank={{ill|Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社#指定護国神社}}・{{ill|Beppyo Shrines|ja|別表神社}} |founded=[[1941]] (Showa 16) |main Hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |festival=[[April 29]]・[[November 3]] |map=Japan Yamaguchi#Japan }} '''Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine'''（山口縣護國神社{{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|山口縣護國神社}}）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the war dead from Yamaguchi Prefecture who perished in national crises since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. This includes notable figures such as {{ill|Yoshida Shoin|ja|吉田松陰}}, {{ill|Kusaka Genzui|ja|久坂玄瑞}}, {{ill|Kijima Matabei|ja|来島又兵衛}}, {{ill|Omura Masujiro|ja|大村益次郎}}, {{ill|Takasugi Shinsaku|ja|高杉晋作}}, and {{ill|Gessho|ja|月性}}. After {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, Self-Defense Force personnel from Yamaguchi Prefecture who died in the line of duty have also been enshrined. == History == In 1903 (Meiji 36), the {{ill|Bocho Yasukenkai|ja|防長靖献会}} was established in Yamaguchi Prefecture, and memorial services were held at the Sakura Batake parade ground (now the training ground for the {{ill|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|ja|陸上自衛隊}} {{ill|Yamaguchi Garrison|ja|山口駐屯地}}) adjacent to the current location. In 1939 (Showa 14), it was decided to establish a Gokoku Shrine in Yamaguchi Prefecture. The shrine was completed at its current location in August 1941 (Showa 16). It was designated as an Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine, and on November 26 of the same year, 7159 spirits from Yamaguchi Prefecture who were enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} were transferred here. The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : {{ill|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|ja|山口駅}} ({{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} (JR West) {{ill|Yamaguchi Line|ja|山口線}}) - 10 minutes by car == Surroundings == * {{ill|Ima Hachiman Shrine|ja|今八幡宮}} * {{ill|Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|ja|常栄寺}} * {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}} * {{ill|Saikotei|ja|山口市菜香亭}} * {{ill|Kumotani-an|ja|雲谷庵}} ruins * {{ill|Rurikō-ji|ja|瑠璃光寺}} * {{ill|Dōshun-ji|ja|洞春寺}}, Nomura Art Museum == References == {{Reflist}} '''Notes''' {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} '''Citations''' {{Reflist}} '''References''' [[ja:山口縣護國神社]] == See also == * {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道}} * {{ill|War memorial|ja|戦争祈念施設}} * {{ill|Self-Defense Forces Gokoku Shrine Enshrinement Incident|ja|自衛官護国神社合祀事件}} == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4864182037}} * [http://yamaguchi-gokoku.jp/ Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{citation needed|Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Yamaguchi City Buildings]] [[Category:Yamaguchi City History]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:bump]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:qq\]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \[[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:tier 5]][[Category: 5]][[Category:qq 2]]\[[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:tier 5]][[Category: 5]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

This includes notable figures such as {{ill|Yoshida Shoin|ja|吉田松陰}}, {{ill|Kusaka Genzui|ja|久坂玄瑞}}, {{ill|Kijima Matabei|ja|来島又兵衛}}, {{ill|Omura Masujiro|ja|大村益次郎}}, {{ill|Takasugi Shinsaku|ja|高杉晋作}}, and {{ill|Gessho|ja|月性}}. After {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, Self-Defense Force personnel from Yamaguchi Prefecture who died in the line of duty have also been enshrined. == History == In 1903 (Meiji 36), the {{ill|Bocho Yasukenkai|ja|防長靖献会}} was established in Yamaguchi Prefecture, and memorial services were held at the Sakura Batake parade ground (now the training ground for the {{ill|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|ja|陸上自衛隊}} {{ill|Yamaguchi Garrison|ja|山口駐屯地}}) adjacent to the current location. In 1939 (Showa 14), it was decided to establish a Gokoku Shrine in Yamaguchi Prefecture. The shrine was completed at its current location in August 1941 (Showa 16). It was designated as an Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine, and on November 26 of the same year, 7159 spirits from Yamaguchi Prefecture who were enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} were transferred here. The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : {{ill|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|ja|山口駅}} ({{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} (JR West) {{ill|Yamaguchi Line|ja|山口線}}) - 10 minutes by car == Surroundings == * {{ill|Ima Hachiman Shrine|ja|今八幡宮}} * {{ill|Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|ja|常栄寺}} * {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}} * {{ill|Saikotei|ja|山口市菜香亭}} * {{ill|Kumotani-an|ja|雲谷庵}} ruins * {{ill|Rurikō-ji|ja|瑠璃光寺}} * {{ill|Dōshun-ji|ja|洞春寺}}, Nomura Art Museum == References == {{Reflist}} '''Notes''' {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} '''Citations''' {{Reflist}} '''References''' [[ja:山口縣護國神社]] == See also == * {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道}} * {{ill|War memorial|ja|戦争祈念施設}} * {{ill|Self-Defense Forces Gokoku Shrine Enshrinement Incident|ja|自衛官護国神社合祀事件}} == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4864182037}} * [http://yamaguchi-gokoku.jp/ Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{citation needed|Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Yamaguchi City Buildings]] [[Category:Yamaguchi City History]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] {{citation needed|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]]{{citation needed|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]]{{citation needed|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:11\]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \[[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:tier 5]][[Category: 5]][[Category:11]]\[[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:tier 5]][[Category: 5]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|marker=place-of-worship}} |location={{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}} {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市}} Hirano 2-2-1 |ISO=JP-35 |latitude degrees=34 |rank minutes=11 |rank seconds=37.5 |N/S=N |longitude degrees=131 |longitude minutes=29 |longitude seconds=25.8 |E/W=E |deity=National martyrs related to Yamaguchi Prefecture |rank={{ill|Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社#指定護国神社}}・{{ill|Beppyo Shrines|ja|別表神社}} |founded=[[1941]] (Showa 16) |main Hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |festival=[[April 29]]・[[November 3]] |map=Japan Yamaguchi#Japan }} '''Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine'''（山口縣護國神社{{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|山口縣護國神社}}）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the war dead from Yamaguchi Prefecture who perished in national crises since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. This includes notable figures such as {{ill|Yoshida Shoin|ja|吉田松陰}}, {{ill|Kusaka Genzui|ja|久坂玄瑞}}, {{ill|Kijima Matabei|ja|来島又兵衛}}, {{ill|Omura Masujiro|ja|大村益次郎}}, {{ill|Takasugi Shinsaku|ja|高杉晋作}}, and {{ill|Gessho|ja|月性}}. After {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, Self-Defense Force personnel from Yamaguchi Prefecture who died in the line of duty have also been enshrined. == History == In 1903 (Meiji 36), the {{ill|Bocho Yasukenkai|ja|防長靖献会}} was established in Yamaguchi Prefecture, and memorial services were held at the Sakura Batake parade ground (now the training ground for the {{ill|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|ja|陸上自衛隊}} {{ill|Yamaguchi Garrison|ja|山口駐屯地}}) adjacent to the current location. In 1939 (Showa 14), it was decided to establish a Gokoku Shrine in Yamaguchi Prefecture. The shrine was completed at its current location in August 1941 (Showa 16). It was designated as an Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine, and on November 26 of the same year, 7159 spirits from Yamaguchi Prefecture who were enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} were transferred here. The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : {{ill|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|ja|山口駅}} ({{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} (JR West) {{ill|Yamaguchi Line|ja|山口線}}) - 10 minutes by car == Surroundings == * {{ill|Ima Hachiman Shrine|ja|今八幡宮}} * {{ill|Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|ja|常栄寺}} * {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}} * {{ill|Saikotei|ja|山口市菜香亭}} * {{ill|Kumotani-an|ja|雲谷庵}} ruins * {{ill|Rurikō-ji|ja|瑠璃光寺}} * {{ill|Dōshun-ji|ja|洞春寺}}, Nomura Art Museum == References == {{Reflist}} '''Notes''' {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} '''Citations''' {{Reflist}} '''References''' [[ja:山口縣護國神社]] == See also == * {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道}} * {{ill|War memorial|ja|戦争祈念施設}} * {{ill|Self-Defense Forces Gokoku Shrine Enshrinement Incident|ja|自衛官護国神社合祀事件}} == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4864182037}} * [http://yamaguchi-gokoku.jp/ Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{citation needed|Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Yamaguchi City Buildings]] [[Category:Yamaguchi City History]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] {{citation needed|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]]{{citation needed|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]]{{citation needed|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:11\]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \[[Category:Completion tier 7]][[Category: 5]][[Category:11]]\[[Category:Completion tier 7]][[Category: 5]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:New]]

{{Cite web|date=2011th year 1月}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine |image=[[File:Yamaguchi_gokoku_shrine.jpg|270px]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|marker=place-of-worship}} |location={{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}} {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市}} Hirano 2-2-1 |ISO=JP-35 |latitude degrees=34 |rank minutes=11 |rank seconds=37.5 |N/S=N |longitude degrees=131 |longitude minutes=29 |longitude seconds=25.8 |E/W=E |deity=National martyrs related to Yamaguchi Prefecture |rank={{ill|Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社#指定護国神社}}・{{ill|Beppyo Shrines|ja|別表神社}} |founded=[[1941]] (Showa 16) |main Hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |festival=[[April 29]]・[[November 3]] |map=Japan Yamaguchi#Japan }} '''Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine'''（山口縣護國神社{{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|山口縣護國神社}}）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the war dead from Yamaguchi Prefecture who perished in national crises since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}. This includes notable figures such as {{ill|Yoshida Shoin|ja|吉田松陰}}, {{ill|Kusaka Genzui|ja|久坂玄瑞}}, {{ill|Kijima Matabei|ja|来島又兵衛}}, {{ill|Omura Masujiro|ja|大村益次郎}}, {{ill|Takasugi Shinsaku|ja|高杉晋作}}, and {{ill|Gessho|ja|月性}}. After {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, Self-Defense Force personnel from Yamaguchi Prefecture who died in the line of duty have also been enshrined. == History == In 1903 (Meiji 36), the {{ill|Bocho Yasukenkai|ja|防長靖献会}} was established in Yamaguchi Prefecture, and memorial services were held at the Sakura Batake parade ground (now the training ground for the {{ill|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|ja|陸上自衛隊}} {{ill|Yamaguchi Garrison|ja|山口駐屯地}}) adjacent to the current location. In 1939 (Showa 14), it was decided to establish a Gokoku Shrine in Yamaguchi Prefecture. The shrine was completed at its current location in August 1941 (Showa 16). It was designated as an Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine, and on November 26 of the same year, 7159 spirits from Yamaguchi Prefecture who were enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} were transferred here. The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : {{ill|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|ja|山口駅}} ({{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} (JR West) {{ill|Yamaguchi Line|ja|山口線}}) - 10 minutes by car == Surroundings == * {{ill|Ima Hachiman Shrine|ja|今八幡宮}} * {{ill|Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|ja|常栄寺}} * {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}} * {{ill|Saikotei|ja|山口市菜香亭}} * {{ill|Kumotani-an|ja|雲谷庵}} ruins * {{ill|Rurikō-ji|ja|瑠璃光寺}} * {{ill|Dōshun-ji|ja|洞春寺}}, Nomura Art Museum == References == {{Reflist}} '''Notes''' {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} '''Citations''' {{Reflist}} '''References''' == See also == * {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道}} * {{ill|War memorial|ja|戦争祈念施設}} * {{ill|Self-Defense Forces Gokoku Shrine Enshrinement Incident|ja|自衛官護国神社合祀事件}} == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4864182037}} * [http://yamaguchi-gokoku.jp/ Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{citation needed|Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{citation needed|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}}{{citation needed|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}}{{citation needed|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Yamaguchi City Buildings]] [[Category:Yamaguchi City History]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:11\]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category: tier 7]] [[Category: 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:山口縣護國神社]] [[sn:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]]

The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : {{ill|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|ja|山口駅|12=draft}} ({{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} (JR West) {{ill|Yamaguchi Line|ja|山口線|12=draft}}) - 10 minutes by car == Surroundings == * {{ill|Ima Hachiman Shrine|ja|今八幡宮|12=draft}} * {{ill|Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|ja|常栄寺|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Saikotei|ja|山口市菜香亭|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kumotani-an|ja|雲谷庵|12=draft}} ruins * {{ill|Rurikō-ji|ja|瑠璃光寺|12=draft}} * {{ill|Dōshun-ji|ja|洞春寺|12=draft}}, Nomura Art Museum == References == {{Reflist}} '''Notes''' {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} '''Citations''' {{Reflist}} '''References''' == See also == * {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道|12=draft}} * {{ill|War memorial|ja|戦争祈念施設|12=draft}} * {{ill|Self-Defense Forces Gokoku Shrine Enshrinement Incident|ja|自衛官護国神社合祀事件|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4864182037}} * [http://yamaguchi-gokoku.jp/ Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{citation needed|Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{citation needed|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}}{{citation needed|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}}{{citation needed|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Yamaguchi City Buildings]] [[Category:Yamaguchi City History]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:11\]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category: 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:山口縣護國神社]] [[sn:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]]

This includes notable figures such as {{ill|Yoshida Shoin|ja|吉田松陰|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kusaka Genzui|ja|久坂玄瑞|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kijima Matabei|ja|来島又兵衛|12=draft}}, {{ill|Omura Masujiro|ja|大村益次郎|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takasugi Shinsaku|ja|高杉晋作|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Gessho|ja|月性|12=draft}}. {{Cite web|date=2011th year 1月}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine |image=[[File:Yamaguchi_gokoku_shrine.jpg|270px]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|marker=place-of-worship}} |location={{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市|12=draft}} Hirano 2-2-1 |ISO=JP-35 |latitude degrees=34 |rank minutes=11 |rank seconds=37.5 |N/S=N |longitude degrees=131 |longitude minutes=29 |longitude seconds=25.8 |E/W=E |deity=National martyrs related to Yamaguchi Prefecture |rank={{ill|Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社#指定護国神社|12=draft}}・{{ill|Beppyo Shrines|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} |founded=[[1941]] (Showa 16) |main Hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |festival=[[April 29]]・[[November 3]] |map=Japan Yamaguchi#Japan }} '''Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine'''（山口縣護國神社{{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|山口縣護國神社|12=draft}}）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|12=draft}}) located in {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|ja|山口県|12=draft}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the war dead from Yamaguchi Prefecture who perished in national crises since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}. This includes notable figures such as {{ill|Yoshida Shoin|ja|吉田松陰|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kusaka Genzui|ja|久坂玄瑞|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kijima Matabei|ja|来島又兵衛|12=draft}}, {{ill|Omura Masujiro|ja|大村益次郎|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takasugi Shinsaku|ja|高杉晋作|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Gessho|ja|月性|12=draft}}. After {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|12=draft}}, Self-Defense Force personnel from Yamaguchi Prefecture who died in the line of duty have also been enshrined. == History == In 1903 (Meiji 36), the {{ill|Bocho Yasukenkai|ja|防長靖献会|12=draft}} was established in Yamaguchi Prefecture, and memorial services were held at the Sakura Batake parade ground (now the training ground for the {{ill|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|ja|陸上自衛隊|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamaguchi Garrison|ja|山口駐屯地|12=draft}}) adjacent to the current location. In 1939 (Showa 14), it was decided to establish a Gokoku Shrine in Yamaguchi Prefecture. The shrine was completed at its current location in August 1941 (Showa 16). It was designated as an Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine, and on November 26 of the same year, 7159 spirits from Yamaguchi Prefecture who were enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|1=Yasukuni Shrine|12=draft}} were transferred here. The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : {{ill|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|ja|山口駅|12=draft}} ({{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} (JR West) {{ill|Yamaguchi Line|ja|山口線|12=draft}}) - 10 minutes by car == Surroundings == * {{ill|Ima Hachiman Shrine|ja|今八幡宮|12=draft}} * {{ill|Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|ja|常栄寺|12=draft}} * {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Saikotei|ja|山口市菜香亭|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kumotani-an|ja|雲谷庵|12=draft}} ruins * {{ill|Rurikō-ji|ja|瑠璃光寺|12=draft}} * {{ill|Dōshun-ji|ja|洞春寺|12=draft}}, Nomura Art Museum == References == {{Reflist}} '''Notes''' {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} '''Citations''' {{Reflist}} '''References''' == See also == * {{ill|State Shinto|ja|国家神道|12=draft}} * {{ill|War memorial|ja|戦争祈念施設|12=draft}} * {{ill|Self-Defense Forces Gokoku Shrine Enshrinement Incident|ja|自衛官護国神社合祀事件|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4864182037}} * [http://yamaguchi-gokoku.jp/ Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{citation needed|Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{citation needed|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}}{{citation needed|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}}{{citation needed|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Yamaguchi Prefecture Shrines]] [[Category:Yamaguchi City Buildings]] [[Category:Yamaguchi City History]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category: 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[sn:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[sn:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:山口縣護國神社]] [[sn:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite web|date=2011th year 1月}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine |image=[[File:Yamaguchi_gokoku_shrine.jpg|270px]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|marker=place-of-worship}} |location=[[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]] Hirano 2-2-1 |ISO=JP-35 |latitude degrees=34 |Rank minutes=11 |Rank seconds=37.5 |N/S=N |longitude degrees=131 |longitude minutes=29 |longitude seconds=25.8 |E/W=E |deity=National martyrs related to Yamaguchi Prefecture |Rank={{ill|Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社#指定護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}・[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] |founded=[[1941]] (Showa 16) |main Hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |festival=[[:en:April 29|April 29]]・[[:en:November 3|November 3]] |map=Japan Yamaguchi#Japan }} '''Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine'''（山口縣護國神社{{ill|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine|lt=Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|山口縣護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=山口県護国神社}}）is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]]) located in [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the war dead from Yamaguchi Prefecture who perished in national crises since the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]]. This includes notable figures such as [[:en:Yoshida Shōin|Yoshida Shoin]], [[:en:Kusaka Genzui|Kusaka Genzui]], [[:en:Kijima Matabē|Kijima Matabei]], [[:en:Ōmura Masujirō|Omura Masujiro]], [[:en:Takasugi Shinsaku|Takasugi Shinsaku]], and {{ill|Gessho|ja|月性|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gessho|qq=}}. After [[:en:World War II|World War II]], Self-Defense Force personnel from Yamaguchi Prefecture who died in the line of duty have also been enshrined. == History == In 1903 (Meiji 36), the {{ill|Bocho Yasukenkai|ja|防長靖献会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bocho Yasukenkai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was established in Yamaguchi Prefecture, and memorial services were held at the Sakura Batake parade ground (now the training ground for the [[:en:Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force]] {{ill|Yamaguchi Garrison|ja|山口駐屯地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Garrison|qq=}}) adjacent to the current location. In 1939 (Showa 14), it was decided to establish a Gokoku Shrine in Yamaguchi Prefecture. The shrine was completed at its current location in August 1941 (Showa 16). It was designated as an Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine, and on November 26 of the same year, 7159 spirits from Yamaguchi Prefecture who were enshrined at [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]] were transferred here. The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : [[:en:Yamaguchi Station|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi Prefecture)]] ([[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] (JR West) [[:en:Yamaguchi Line|Yamaguchi Line]]) - 10 minutes by car == Surroundings == * {{ill|Ima Hachiman Shrine|ja|今八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ima Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|ja|常栄寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|qq=}} * {{ill|Saikotei|ja|山口市菜香亭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikotei|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumotani-an|ja|雲谷庵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumotani-an|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ruins * [[:en:Rurikō-ji|Rurikō-ji]] * {{ill|Dōshun-ji|ja|洞春寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōshun-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nomura Art Museum == References == {{Reflist}} '''Notes''' {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} '''Citations''' {{Reflist}} '''References''' == See also == * [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] * {{ill|War memorial|ja|戦争祈念施設|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/War memorial|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=戦争記念施設}} * {{ill|Self-Defense Forces Gokoku Shrine Enshrinement Incident|ja|自衛官護国神社合祀事件|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Self-Defense Forces Gokoku Shrine Enshrinement Incident|qq=}} == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4864182037}} * [http://yamaguchi-gokoku.jp/ Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ }} {{translated page|ja|山口縣護國神社|version=10244808|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[sn:Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:山口縣護國神社]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|marker=place-of-worship}} |location=[[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]] Hirano 2-2-1 |ISO=JP-35 |latitude degrees=34 |Rank minutes=11 |Rank seconds=37.5 |N/S=N |longitude degrees=131 |longitude minutes=29 |longitude seconds=25.8 |E/W=E |deity=National martyrs related to Yamaguchi Prefecture |Rank={{ill|Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社#指定護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|qq=|1=gokoku shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1534477}}・[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] |founded=[[1941]] (Showa 16) |main Hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |festival=[[:en:April 29|April 29]]・[[:en:November 3|November 3]] |map=Japan Yamaguchi#Japan }} '''Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine'''（山口縣護國神社{{ill|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine|lt=Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|山口縣護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=山口県護国神社}}）is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]]) located in [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the war dead from Yamaguchi Prefecture who perished in national crises since the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]]. This includes notable figures such as [[:en:Yoshida Shōin|Yoshida Shoin]], [[:en:Kusaka Genzui|Kusaka Genzui]], [[:en:Kijima Matabē|Kijima Matabei]], [[:en:Ōmura Masujirō|Omura Masujiro]], [[:en:Takasugi Shinsaku|Takasugi Shinsaku]], and {{ill|Gessho|ja|月性|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gessho|qq=|1=Gesshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6605593}}. After [[:en:World War II|World War II]], Self-Defense Force personnel from Yamaguchi Prefecture who died in the line of duty have also been enshrined. == History == In 1903 (Meiji 36), the {{ill|Bocho Yasukenkai|ja|防長靖献会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bocho Yasukenkai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was established in Yamaguchi Prefecture, and memorial services were held at the Sakura Batake parade ground (now the training ground for the [[:en:Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force]] {{ill|Yamaguchi Garrison|ja|山口駐屯地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Garrison|qq=|1=Camp Yamaguchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467540}}) adjacent to the current location. In 1939 (Showa 14), it was decided to establish a Gokoku Shrine in Yamaguchi Prefecture. The shrine was completed at its current location in August 1941 (Showa 16). It was designated as an Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine, and on November 26 of the same year, 7159 spirits from Yamaguchi Prefecture who were enshrined at [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]] were transferred here. The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : [[:en:Yamaguchi Station|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi Prefecture)]] ([[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] (JR West) [[:en:Yamaguchi Line|Yamaguchi Line]]) - 10 minutes by car == Surroundings == * {{ill|Ima Hachiman Shrine|ja|今八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ima Hachiman Shrine|qq=|1=Ima-hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65248550}} * {{ill|Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|ja|常栄寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|qq=|1=Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481411}} * {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|qq=}} * revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2011年1月}} {{神社 |名称=山口縣護國神社 |画像=[[File:Yamaguchi_gokoku_shrine.jpg|270px]]
拝殿
{{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|marker=religious-shinto}} |所在地=[[山口県]][[山口市]]平野二丁目2番1号 |ISO = JP-35 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 11 | 緯度秒 = 37.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 131 |経度分 = 29 | 経度秒 = 25.8 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |祭神=山口県関係の国事殉難者 |社格=内務大臣指定護国神社・別表神社 |創建=[[1941年]]（昭和16年） |本殿=一間社[[流造]] |例祭=[[4月29日]]・[[11月3日]] |地図 = Japan Yamaguchi#Japan }} '''山口縣護國神社'''（やまぐちけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[山口県]][[山口市]]にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）である。 == 祭神 == 山口県関係の[[明治維新]]以降の国難に殉じた戦没者を祀る。この中には、[[吉田松陰]]、[[久坂玄瑞]]、[[来島又兵衛]]、[[大村益次郎]]、[[高杉晋作]]、[[月性]]も含まれている。 [[第二次世界大戦]]後は、山口県出身の殉職[[自衛官]]も合祀している。 == 歴史 == 山口県では、[[1903年]]（[[明治]]36年）に防長靖献会が設立され、現鎮座地の南に隣接する桜畠練兵場（現 [[陸上自衛隊]][[山口駐屯地]]訓練場）で招魂祭が斎行されていた。 [[1939年]]（[[昭和]]14年）、山口県にも[[護国神社]]を創建することとなり、[[1941年]]（昭和16年）8月、現在地に社殿が竣工した。内務大臣から指定護国神社の指定を受け、同年11月26日に[[靖国神社]]の祭神となっていた山口県出身の戦没者7159柱を合祀、翌27日に鎮座祭が斎行された。 == 所在地情報 == ; 所在地 :* 山口県山口市平野二丁目2番1号 ; 交通 :* 鉄道 :** [[山口駅 (山口県)|山口駅]]（[[西日本旅客鉄道]]（JR西日本）[[山口線]]） - 車で10分 == 周辺 == * [[今八幡宮]] * [[常栄寺 (山口市)|常栄寺]] * [[豊榮神社・野田神社]] * [[山口市菜香亭]] * [[雲谷庵]]跡 * [[瑠璃光寺]] * [[洞春寺]]、のむら美術館 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''注釈''' {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} '''出典''' {{Reflist}} '''参考文献''' == 関連項目 == * [[国家神道]] * [[戦争祈念施設]] * [[自衛官護国神社合祀事件]] == 外部リンク == {{Osm box|n|4864182037}} * [http://yamaguchi-gokoku.jp/ 山口縣護國神社] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまくちけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口市の建築物]] [[Category:山口市の歴史]] [[Category:護国神社]] [[Category:別表神社]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|marker=place-of-worship}} |location=[[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]] Hirano 2-2-1 |ISO=JP-35 |latitude degrees=34 |Rank minutes=11 |Rank seconds=37.5 |N/S=N |longitude degrees=131 |longitude minutes=29 |longitude seconds=25.8 |E/W=E |deity=National martyrs related to Yamaguchi Prefecture |Rank={{ill|Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社#指定護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|qq=|1=gokoku shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1534477}}・[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] |founded=[[1941]] (Showa 16) |main Hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |festival=[[:en:April 29|April 29]]・[[:en:November 3|November 3]] |map=Japan Yamaguchi#Japan }} '''Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine'''（山口縣護國神社{{ill|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine|lt=Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|山口縣護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=山口県護国神社}}）is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]]) located in [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the war dead from Yamaguchi Prefecture who perished in national crises since the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]]. This includes notable figures such as [[:en:Yoshida Shōin|Yoshida Shoin]], [[:en:Kusaka Genzui|Kusaka Genzui]], [[:en:Kijima Matabē|Kijima Matabei]], [[:en:Ōmura Masujirō|Omura Masujiro]], [[:en:Takasugi Shinsaku|Takasugi Shinsaku]], and {{ill|Gessho|ja|月性|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gessho|qq=|1=Gesshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6605593}}. After [[:en:World War II|World War II]], Self-Defense Force personnel from Yamaguchi Prefecture who died in the line of duty have also been enshrined. == History == In 1903 (Meiji 36), the {{ill|Bocho Yasukenkai|ja|防長靖献会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bocho Yasukenkai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was established in Yamaguchi Prefecture, and memorial services were held at the Sakura Batake parade ground (now the training ground for the [[:en:Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force]] {{ill|Yamaguchi Garrison|ja|山口駐屯地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Garrison|qq=|1=Camp Yamaguchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467540}}) adjacent to the current location. In 1939 (Showa 14), it was decided to establish a Gokoku Shrine in Yamaguchi Prefecture. The shrine was completed at its current location in August 1941 (Showa 16). It was designated as an Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine, and on November 26 of the same year, 7159 spirits from Yamaguchi Prefecture who were enshrined at [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]] were transferred here. The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : [[:en:Yamaguchi
