[[sn:commons:Category:Yamaguchiken gokoku-jinja]][[ja:山口県護国神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite web|date=2011th year 1月}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine |image=[[File:Yamaguchi_gokoku_shrine.jpg|270px]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|marker=place-of-worship}} |location=[[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]] Hirano 2-2-1 |ISO=JP-35 |latitude degrees=34 |Rank minutes=11 |Rank seconds=37.5 |N/S=N |longitude degrees=131 |longitude minutes=29 |longitude seconds=25.8 |E/W=E |deity=National martyrs related to Yamaguchi Prefecture |Rank={{ill|Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社#指定護国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|qq=|1=gokoku shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1534477}}・[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] |founded=[[1941]] (Showa 16) |main Hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |festival=[[:en:April 29|April 29]]・[[:en:November 3|November 3]] |map=Japan Yamaguchi#Japan }} '''Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine'''（山口縣護國神社{{ill|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine|lt=Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|山口縣護國神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=山口県護国神社}}）is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]]) located in [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the war dead from Yamaguchi Prefecture who perished in national crises since the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]]. This includes notable figures such as [[:en:Yoshida Shōin|Yoshida Shoin]], [[:en:Kusaka Genzui|Kusaka Genzui]], [[:en:Kijima Matabē|Kijima Matabei]], [[:en:Ōmura Masujirō|Omura Masujiro]], [[:en:Takasugi Shinsaku|Takasugi Shinsaku]], and {{ill|Gessho|ja|月性|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gessho|qq=|1=Gesshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6605593}}. After [[:en:World War II|World War II]], Self-Defense Force personnel from Yamaguchi Prefecture who died in the line of duty have also been enshrined. == History == In 1903 (Meiji 36), the {{ill|Bocho Yasukenkai|ja|防長靖献会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bocho Yasukenkai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was established in Yamaguchi Prefecture, and memorial services were held at the Sakura Batake parade ground (now the training ground for the [[:en:Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force]] {{ill|Yamaguchi Garrison|ja|山口駐屯地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Garrison|qq=|1=Camp Yamaguchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467540}}) adjacent to the current location. In 1939 (Showa 14), it was decided to establish a Gokoku Shrine in Yamaguchi Prefecture. The shrine was completed at its current location in August 1941 (Showa 16). It was designated as an Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine, and on November 26 of the same year, 7159 spirits from Yamaguchi Prefecture who were enshrined at [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]] were transferred here. The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : [[:en:Yamaguchi Station|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi Prefecture)]] ([[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] (JR West) [[:en:Yamaguchi Line|Yamaguchi Line]]) - 10 minutes by car == Surroundings It was designated as an Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine, and on November 26 of the same year, 7159 spirits from Yamaguchi Prefecture who were enshrined at [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]] were transferred here. The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : [[:en:Yamaguchi Station|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi Prefecture)]] ([[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] (JR West) [[:en:Yamaguchi Line|Yamaguchi Line]]) - {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|marker=place-of-worship}} |location=[[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]] Hirano 2-2-1 |ISO=JP-35 |latitude degrees=34 |Rank minutes=11 |Rank seconds=37.5 |N/S=N |longitude degrees=131 |longitude minutes=29 |longitude seconds=25.8 |E/W=E |deity=National martyrs related to Yamaguchi Prefecture |Rank={{ill|Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社#指定護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|1=gokoku shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1534477}}・[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] |founded=[[1941]] (Showa 16) |main Hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |festival=[[:en:April 29|April 29]]・[[:en:November 3|November 3]] |map=Japan Yamaguchi#Japan }} '''Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine'''（山口縣護國神社{{ill|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine|lt=Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|山口縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=山口県護国神社}}）is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]]) located in [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the war dead from Yamaguchi Prefecture who perished in national crises since the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]]. This includes notable figures such as [[:en:Yoshida Shōin|Yoshida Shoin]], [[:en:Kusaka Genzui|Kusaka Genzui]], [[:en:Kijima Matabē|Kijima Matabei]], [[:en:Ōmura Masujirō|Omura Masujiro]], [[:en:Takasugi Shinsaku|Takasugi Shinsaku]], and {{ill|Gessho|ja|月性|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gessho|1=Gesshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6605593}}. After [[:en:World War II|World War II]], Self-Defense Force personnel from Yamaguchi Prefecture who died in the line of duty have also been enshrined. == History == In 1903 (Meiji 36), the {{ill|Bocho Yasukenkai|ja|防長靖献会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bocho Yasukenkai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was established in Yamaguchi Prefecture, and memorial services were held at the Sakura Batake parade ground (now the training ground for the [[:en:Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force]] {{ill|Yamaguchi Garrison|ja|山口駐屯地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Garrison|1=Camp Yamaguchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467540}}) adjacent to the current location. In 1939 (Showa 14), it was decided to establish a Gokoku Shrine in Yamaguchi Prefecture. The shrine was completed at its current location in August 1941 (Showa 16). It was designated as an Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine, and on November 26 of the same year, 7159 spirits from Yamaguchi Prefecture who were enshrined at [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]] were transferred here. The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : [[:en:Yamaguchi Station|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi Prefecture)]] ([[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] (JR West) [[:en:Yamaguchi Line|Yamaguchi Line]]) - 10 minutes by car == Surroundings == * {{ill|Ima Hachiman Shrine|ja|今八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ima Hachiman Shrine|1=Ima-hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65248550}} * {{ill|Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi Station|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi Prefecture)]] ([[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] (JR West) [[:en:Yamaguchi Line|Yamaguchi Line]]) - 10 minutes by car == Surroundings [[sn:commons:Category:Yamaguchiken gokoku-jinja]][[ja:山口県護国神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite web|date=2011th year 1月}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine |image=[[File:Yamaguchi_gokoku_shrine.jpg|270px]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|marker=place-of-worship}} |location=[[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]] Hirano 2-2-1 |ISO=JP-35 |latitude degrees=34 |Rank minutes=11 |Rank seconds=37.5 |N/S=N |longitude degrees=131 |longitude minutes=29 |longitude seconds=25.8 |E/W=E |deity=National martyrs related to Yamaguchi Prefecture |Rank={{ill|Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社#指定護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|1=gokoku shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1534477}}・[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] |founded=[[1941]] (Showa 16) |main Hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |festival=[[:en:April 29|April 29]]・[[:en:November 3|November 3]] |map=Japan Yamaguchi#Japan }} '''Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine'''（山口縣護國神社{{ill|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine|lt=Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|山口縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=山口県護国神社}}）is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]]) located in [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the war dead from Yamaguchi Prefecture who perished in national crises since the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]]. This includes notable figures such as [[:en:Yoshida Shōin|Yoshida Shoin]], [[:en:Kusaka Genzui|Kusaka Genzui]], [[:en:Kijima Matabē|Kijima Matabei]], [[:en:Ōmura Masujirō|Omura Masujiro]], [[:en:Takasugi Shinsaku|Takasugi Shinsaku]], and {{ill|Gessho|ja|月性|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gessho|1=Gesshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6605593}}. After [[:en:World War II|World War II]], Self-Defense Force personnel from Yamaguchi Prefecture who died in the line of duty have also been enshrined. == History == In 1903 (Meiji 36), the {{ill|Bocho Yasukenkai|ja|防長靖献会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bocho Yasukenkai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was established in Yamaguchi Prefecture, and memorial services were held at the Sakura Batake parade ground (now the training ground for the [[:en:Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force]] {{ill|Yamaguchi Garrison|ja|山口駐屯地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Garrison|1=Camp Yamaguchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467540}}) adjacent to the current location. In 1939 (Showa 14), it was decided to establish a Gokoku Shrine in Yamaguchi Prefecture. The shrine was completed at its current location in August 1941 (Showa 16). It was designated as an Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine, and on November 26 of the same year, 7159 spirits from Yamaguchi Prefecture who were enshrined at [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]] were transferred here. The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : [[:en:Yamaguchi Station|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi Prefecture)]] ([[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] (JR West) [[:en:Yamaguchi Line|Yamaguchi Line]]) - 10 minutes by car == Surroundings == * {{ill|Ima Hachiman Shrine|ja|今八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ima Hachiman Shrine|1=Ima-hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65248550}} * {{ill|Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|ja|常栄寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|1=Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481411}} * {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|qq=}} * {{ill|Saikotei|ja|山口市菜香亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikotei|1=Saikotei|comment=changed title based on [[sn:commons:Category:Yamaguchiken gokoku-jinja]][[ja:山口県護国神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite web|date=2011th year 1月}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine |image=[[File:Yamaguchi_gokoku_shrine.jpg|270px]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|marker=place-of-worship}} |location=[[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]] Hirano 2-2-1 |ISO=JP-35 |latitude degrees=34 |Rank minutes=11 |Rank seconds=37.5 |N/S=N |longitude degrees=131 |longitude minutes=29 |longitude seconds=25.8 |E/W=E |deity=National martyrs related to Yamaguchi Prefecture |Rank={{ill|Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社#指定護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|1=gokoku shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1534477}}・[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] |founded=[[1941]] (Showa 16) |main Hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |festival=[[:en:April 29|April 29]]・[[:en:November 3|November 3]] |map=Japan Yamaguchi#Japan }} '''Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine'''（山口縣護國神社{{ill|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine|lt=Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|山口縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=山口県護国神社}}）is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]]) located in [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the war dead from Yamaguchi Prefecture who perished in national crises since the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]]. This includes notable figures such as [[:en:Yoshida Shōin|Yoshida Shoin]], [[:en:Kusaka Genzui|Kusaka Genzui]], [[:en:Kijima Matabē|Kijima Matabei]], [[:en:Ōmura Masujirō|Omura Masujiro]], [[:en:Takasugi Shinsaku|Takasugi Shinsaku]], and {{ill|Gessho|ja|月性|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gessho|1=Gesshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6605593}}. After [[:en:World War II|World War II]], Self-Defense Force personnel from Yamaguchi Prefecture who died in the line of duty have also been enshrined. == History == In 1903 (Meiji 36), the {{ill|Bocho Yasukenkai|ja|防長靖献会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bocho Yasukenkai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was established in Yamaguchi Prefecture, and memorial services were held at the Sakura Batake parade ground (now the training ground for the [[:en:Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force]] {{ill|Yamaguchi Garrison|ja|山口駐屯地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Garrison|1=Camp Yamaguchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467540}}) adjacent to the current location. In 1939 (Showa 14), it was decided to establish a Gokoku Shrine in Yamaguchi Prefecture. The shrine was completed at its current location in August 1941 (Showa 16). It was designated as an Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine, and on November 26 of the same year, 7159 spirits from Yamaguchi Prefecture who were enshrined at [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]] were transferred here. The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : [[:en:Yamaguchi Station|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi Prefecture)]] ([[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] (JR West) [[:en:Yamaguchi Line|Yamaguchi Line]]) - 10 minutes by car == Surroundings == * {{ill|Ima Hachiman Shrine|ja|今八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ima Hachiman Shrine|1=Ima-hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65248550}} * {{ill|Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|ja|常栄寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|1=Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481411}} * {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|qq=}} * {{ill|Saikotei|ja|山口市菜香亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikotei|1=Saikotei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18458034}} * {{ill|Kumotani-an|ja|雲谷庵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumotani-an|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ruins * [[:en:Rurikō-ji|Rurikō-ji]] * memorial|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q575759}} * {{ill|Self-Defense Forces Gokoku Shrine Enshrinement Incident|ja|自衛官護国神社合祀事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Self-Defense Forces Gokoku Shrine Enshrinement Incident|1=Self-Defense Forces Gokoku Shrine Enshrinement Incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613016}} == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4864182037}} * After [[:en:World War II|World War II]], Self-Defense Force personnel from Yamaguchi Prefecture who died in the line of duty have also been enshrined. == History == In 1903 (Meiji 36), the {{ill|Bocho Yasukenkai|ja|防長靖献会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bocho Yasukenkai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was established in Yamaguchi Prefecture, and memorial services were held at the Sakura Batake parade ground (now the training ground for the [[:en:Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force]] {{ill|Yamaguchi Garrison|ja|山口駐屯地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Garrison|1=Camp Yamaguchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467540}}) adjacent to the current location. In 1939 (Showa 14), it was decided to establish a Gokoku Shrine in Yamaguchi Prefecture. The shrine was completed at its current location in August 1941 (Showa 16). It was designated as an Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine, and on November 26 of the same year, 7159 spirits from Yamaguchi Prefecture who were enshrined at [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]] were transferred here. The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : [[:en:Yamaguchi Station|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi Prefecture)]] ([[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] (JR West) [[:en:Yamaguchi Line|Yamaguchi Line]]) - 10 minutes by car == Surroundings == * {{ill|Ima Hachiman Shrine|ja|今八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ima Hachiman Shrine|1=Ima-hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65248550}} * {{ill|Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|ja|常栄寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|1=Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481411}} * {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|qq=}} * {{ill|Saikotei|ja|山口市菜香亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikotei|1=Saikotei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18458034}} * {{ill|Kumotani-an|ja|雲谷庵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumotani-an|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ruins * [[:en:Rurikō-ji|Rurikō-ji]] * {{ill|Dōshun-ji|ja|洞春寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōshun-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nomura Art Museum == References == {{Reflist}} '''Notes''' {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} '''Citations''' {{Reflist}} '''References''' == See also == * [[:en:State Shinto|State Shinto]] * {{ill|War memorial|ja|戦争祈念施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/War memorial|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=戦争記念施設|1=war memorial|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q575759}} * {{ill|Self-Defense Forces Gokoku Shrine Enshrinement Incident|ja|自衛官護国神社合祀事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Self-Defense Forces Gokoku Shrine Enshrinement Incident|1=Self-Defense Forces Gokoku Shrine Enshrinement Incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613016}} == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4864182037}} * [http://yamaguchi-gokoku.jp/ Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[ja:山口県護国神社]] {{translated page|ja|山口県護国神社|version=102012646|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11467121}}

[[co:commons:Category:Yamaguchiken gokoku-jinja]][[ja:山口県護国神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Yamaguchiken gokoku-jinja]][[ja:山口県護国神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yamaguchiken gokoku-jinja]][[ja:山口県護国神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite web|date=2011th year 1月}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine |image=[[File:Yamaguchi_gokoku_shrine.jpg|270px]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|marker=place-of-worship}} |location=[[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]] Hirano 2-2-1 |ISO=JP-35 |latitude degrees=34 |Rank minutes=11 |Rank seconds=37.5 |N/S=N |longitude degrees=131 |longitude minutes=29 |longitude seconds=25.8 |E/W=E |deity=National martyrs related to Yamaguchi Prefecture |Rank={{ill|Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社#指定護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|1=gokoku shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1534477}}・[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] |founded=[[1941]] (Showa 16) |main Hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |festival=[[:en:April 29|April 29]]・[[:en:November 3|November 3]] |map=Japan Yamaguchi#Japan }} '''Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine'''（山口縣護國神社{{ill|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine|lt=Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|山口縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=山口県護国神社}}）is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]]) located in [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the war dead from Yamaguchi Prefecture who perished in national crises since the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]]. This includes notable figures such as [[:en:Yoshida Shōin|Yoshida Shoin]], [[:en:Kusaka Genzui|Kusaka Genzui]], [[:en:Kijima Matabē|Kijima Matabei]], [[:en:Ōmura Masujirō|Omura Masujiro]], [[:en:Takasugi Shinsaku|Takasugi Shinsaku]], and {{ill|Gessho|ja|月性|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gessho|1=Gesshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6605593}}. After [[:en:World War II|World War II]], Self-Defense Force personnel from Yamaguchi Prefecture who died in the line of duty have also been enshrined. == History == In 1903 (Meiji 36), the {{ill|Bocho Yasukenkai|ja|防長靖献会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bocho Yasukenkai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was established in Yamaguchi Prefecture, and memorial services were held at the Sakura Batake parade ground (now the training ground for the [[:en:Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force]] {{ill|Yamaguchi Garrison|ja|山口駐屯地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Garrison|1=Camp Yamaguchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467540}}) adjacent to the current location. In 1939 (Showa 14), it was decided to establish a Gokoku Shrine in Yamaguchi Prefecture. The shrine was completed at its current location in August 1941 (Showa 16). It was designated as an Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine, and on November 26 of the same year, 7159 spirits from Yamaguchi Prefecture who were enshrined at [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]] were transferred here. The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : [[:en:Yamaguchi Station|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi Prefecture)]] ([[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] (JR West) [[:en:Yamaguchi Line|Yamaguchi Line]]) - 10 minutes by car == Surroundings == * {{ill|Ima Hachiman Shrine|ja|今八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ima Hachiman Shrine|1=Ima-hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65248550}} * {{ill|Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|ja|常栄寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|1=Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481411}} * {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|marker=place-of-worship}} |location={{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|WD=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}} {{ill|Yamaguchi (city)|en|Yamaguchi (city)|WD=Q207876|lt=Yamaguchi City}} Hirano 2-2-1 |ISO=JP-35 |latitude degrees=34 |Rank minutes=11 |Rank seconds=37.5 |N/S=N |longitude degrees=131 |longitude minutes=29 |longitude seconds=25.8 |E/W=E |deity=National martyrs related to Yamaguchi Prefecture |Rank={{ill|Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社#指定護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|1=gokoku shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1534477}}・{{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine}} |founded=[[1941]] (Showa 16) |main Hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |festival={{ill|April 29|en|April 29|WD=Q2535|lt=April 29}}・{{ill|November 3|en|November 3|WD=Q2995|lt=November 3}} |map=Japan Yamaguchi#Japan }} '''Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine'''（山口縣護國神社{{ill|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine|lt=Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|山口縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=山口県護国神社}}）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine}}) located in {{ill|Yamaguchi (city)|en|Yamaguchi (city)|WD=Q207876|lt=Yamaguchi City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|WD=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the war dead from Yamaguchi Prefecture who perished in national crises since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}. This includes notable figures such as {{ill|Yoshida Shōin|en|Yoshida Shōin|WD=Q559747|lt=Yoshida Shoin}}, {{ill|Kusaka Genzui|en|Kusaka Genzui|WD=Q5360999|lt=Kusaka Genzui}}, {{ill|Kijima Matabē|en|Kijima Matabē|WD=Q6406161|lt=Kijima Matabei}}, {{ill|Ōmura Masujirō|en|Ōmura Masujirō|WD=Q385551|lt=Omura Masujiro}}, {{ill|Takasugi Shinsaku|en|Takasugi Shinsaku|WD=Q700310|lt=Takasugi Shinsaku}}, and {{ill|Gessho|ja|月性|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gessho|1=Gesshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6605593}}. After {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II}}, Self-Defense Force personnel from Yamaguchi Prefecture who died in the line of duty have also been enshrined. == History == In 1903 (Meiji 36), the {{ill|Bocho Yasukenkai|ja|防長靖献会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bocho Yasukenkai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was established in Yamaguchi Prefecture, and memorial services were held at the Sakura Batake parade ground (now the training ground for the {{ill|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|en|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|WD=Q889751|lt=Japan Ground Self-Defense Force}} {{ill|Yamaguchi Garrison|ja|山口駐屯地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Garrison|1=Camp Yamaguchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467540}}) adjacent to the current location. In 1939 (Showa 14), it was decided to establish a Gokoku Shrine in Yamaguchi Prefecture. The shrine was completed at its current location in August 1941 (Showa 16). It was designated as an Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine, and on November 26 of the same year, 7159 spirits from Yamaguchi Prefecture who were enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine}} were transferred here. The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : {{ill|Yamaguchi Station|en|Yamaguchi Station|WD=Q861526|lt=Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi Prefecture)}} ({{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) {{ill|Yamaguchi Line|en|Yamaguchi Line|WD=Q851226|lt=Yamaguchi Line}}) - 10 minutes by car == Surroundings == * {{ill|Ima Hachiman Shrine|ja|今八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ima Hachiman Shrine|1=Ima-hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65248550}} * {{ill|Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|ja|常栄寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|1=Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481411}} * {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|qq=}} * {{ill|Saikotei|ja|山口市菜香亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikotei|1=Saikotei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18458034}} * {{ill|Kumotani-an|ja|雲谷庵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumotani-an|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ruins * {{ill|Rurikō-ji|en|Rurikō-ji|WD=Q11573988|lt=Rurikō-ji}} * {{ill|Dōshun-ji|ja|洞春寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōshun-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nomura Art Museum == References == {{Reflist}} '''Notes''' {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} '''Citations''' {{Reflist}} '''References''' == See also == * {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto}} * {{ill|War memorial|ja|戦争祈念施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/War memorial|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=戦争記念施設|1=war memorial|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q575759}} * {{ill|Self-Defense Forces Gokoku Shrine Enshrinement Incident|ja|自衛官護国神社合祀事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Self-Defense Forces Gokoku Shrine Enshrinement Incident|1=Self-Defense Forces Gokoku Shrine Enshrinement Incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613016}} == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4864182037}} * [http://yamaguchi-gokoku.jp/ Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ }} {{draft categories| [[ja:山口県護国神社]] {{translated page|ja|山口県護国神社|version=102012646|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11467121}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Yamaguchiken gokoku-jinja]][[ja:山口県護国神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Yamaguchiken gokoku-jinja]][[ja:山口県護国神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yamaguchiken gokoku-jinja]][[ja:山口県護国神社]] {{Cite web|date=2011th year 1月}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine |image=[[File:Yamaguchi_gokoku_shrine.jpg|270px]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|marker=place-of-worship}} |location={{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|WD=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}} {{ill|Yamaguchi (city)|en|Yamaguchi (city)|WD=Q207876|lt=Yamaguchi City}} Hirano 2-2-1 |ISO=JP-35 |latitude degrees=34 |Rank minutes=11 |Rank seconds=37.5 |N/S=N |longitude degrees=131 |longitude minutes=29 |longitude seconds=25.8 |E/W=E |deity=National martyrs related to Yamaguchi Prefecture |Rank={{ill|Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社#指定護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|1=gokoku shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1534477}}・{{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine}} |founded=[[1941]] (Showa 16) |main Hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |festival={{ill|April 29|en|April 29|WD=Q2535|lt=April 29}}・{{ill|November 3|en|November 3|WD=Q2995|lt=November 3}} |map=Japan Yamaguchi#Japan }} '''Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine'''（山口縣護國神社{{ill|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine|lt=Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|山口縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=山口県護国神社}}）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine}}) located in {{ill|Yamaguchi (city)|en|Yamaguchi (city)|WD=Q207876|lt=Yamaguchi City}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|WD=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the war dead from Yamaguchi Prefecture who perished in national crises since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}. This includes notable figures such as {{ill|Yoshida Shōin|en|Yoshida Shōin|WD=Q559747|lt=Yoshida Shoin}}, {{ill|Kusaka Genzui|en|Kusaka Genzui|WD=Q5360999|lt=Kusaka Genzui}}, {{ill|Kijima Matabē|en|Kijima Matabē|WD=Q6406161|lt=Kijima Matabei}}, {{ill|Ōmura Masujirō|en|Ōmura Masujirō|WD=Q385551|lt=Omura Masujiro}}, {{ill|Takasugi Shinsaku|en|Takasugi Shinsaku|WD=Q700310|lt=Takasugi Shinsaku}}, and {{ill|Gessho|ja|月性|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gessho|1=Gesshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6605593}}. After {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II}}, Self-Defense Force personnel from Yamaguchi Prefecture who died in the line of duty have also been enshrined. == History == In 1903 (Meiji 36), the {{ill|Bocho Yasukenkai|ja|防長靖献会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bocho Yasukenkai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was established in Yamaguchi Prefecture, and memorial services were held at the Sakura Batake parade ground (now the training ground for the {{ill|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|en|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|WD=Q889751|lt=Japan Ground Self-Defense Force}} {{ill|Yamaguchi Garrison|ja|山口駐屯地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Garrison|1=Camp Yamaguchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467540}}) adjacent to the current location. In 1939 (Showa 14), it was decided to establish a Gokoku Shrine in Yamaguchi Prefecture. The shrine was completed at its current location in August 1941 (Showa 16). It was designated as an Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine, and on November 26 of the same year, 7159 spirits from Yamaguchi Prefecture who were enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine}} were transferred here. The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : {{ill|Yamaguchi Station|en|Yamaguchi Station|WD=Q861526|lt=Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi Prefecture)}} ({{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) {{ill|Yamaguchi Line|en|Yamaguchi Line|WD=Q851226|lt=Yamaguchi Line}}) - 10 minutes by car == Surroundings == * {{ill|Ima Hachiman Shrine|ja|今八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ima Hachiman Shrine|1=Ima-hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65248550}} * {{ill|Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|ja|常栄寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|1=Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481411}} * {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|qq=}} * {{ill|Saikotei|ja|山口市菜香亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikotei|1=Saikotei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18458034}} * {{ill|Kumotani-an|ja|雲谷庵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumotani-an|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ruins * {{ill|Rurikō-ji|en|Rurikō-ji|WD=Q11573988|lt=Rurikō-ji}} * {{ill|Dōshun-ji|ja|洞春寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōshun-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nomura Art Museum == References == {{Reflist}} '''Notes''' {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} '''Citations''' {{Reflist}} '''References''' == See also == * {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto}} * {{ill|War memorial|ja|戦争祈念施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/War memorial|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=戦争記念施設|1=war memorial|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q575759}} * {{ill|Self-Defense Forces Gokoku Shrine Enshrinement Incident|ja|自衛官護国神社合祀事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Self-Defense Forces Gokoku Shrine Enshrinement Incident|1=Self-Defense Forces Gokoku Shrine Enshrinement Incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613016}} == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4864182037}} * [http://yamaguchi-gokoku.jp/ Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ }} {{draft categories| [[ja:山口県護国神社]] {{translated page|ja|山口県護国神社|version=102012646|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11467121}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 7]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|marker=place-of-worship}} |location={{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|WD=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}} {{ill|Yamaguchi (city)|en|Yamaguchi (city)|WD=Q207876|lt=Yamaguchi City|1=Yamaguchi (city)}} Hirano 2-2-1 |ISO=JP-35 |latitude degrees=34 |Rank minutes=11 |Rank seconds=37.5 |N/S=N |longitude degrees=131 |longitude minutes=29 |longitude seconds=25.8 |E/W=E |deity=National martyrs related to Yamaguchi Prefecture |Rank={{ill|Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社#指定護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1534477}}・{{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} |founded=[[1941]] (Showa 16) |main Hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |festival={{ill|April 29|en|April 29|WD=Q2535|lt=April 29|1=April 29}}・{{ill|November 3|en|November 3|WD=Q2995|lt=November 3|1=November 3}} |map=Japan Yamaguchi#Japan }} '''Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine'''（山口縣護國神社{{ill|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine|lt=Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|山口縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=山口県護国神社}}）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}}) located in {{ill|Yamaguchi (city)|en|Yamaguchi (city)|WD=Q207876|lt=Yamaguchi City|1=Yamaguchi (city)}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|WD=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the war dead from Yamaguchi Prefecture who perished in national crises since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}. This includes notable figures such as {{ill|Yoshida Shōin|en|Yoshida Shōin|WD=Q559747|lt=Yoshida Shoin|1=Yoshida Shōin}}, {{ill|Kusaka Genzui|en|Kusaka Genzui|WD=Q5360999|lt=Kusaka Genzui|1=Kusaka Genzui}}, {{ill|Kijima Matabē|en|Kijima Matabē|WD=Q6406161|lt=Kijima Matabei|1=Kijima Matabē}}, {{ill|Ōmura Masujirō|en|Ōmura Masujirō|WD=Q385551|lt=Omura Masujiro|1=Ōmura Masujirō}}, {{ill|Takasugi Shinsaku|en|Takasugi Shinsaku|WD=Q700310|lt=Takasugi Shinsaku|1=Takasugi Shinsaku}}, and {{ill|Gessho|ja|月性|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gessho|1=Gesshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6605593}}. After {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II|1=World War II}}, Self-Defense Force personnel from Yamaguchi Prefecture who died in the line of duty have also been enshrined. == History == In 1903 (Meiji 36), the {{ill|Bocho Yasukenkai|ja|防長靖献会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bocho Yasukenkai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was established in Yamaguchi Prefecture, and memorial services were held at the Sakura Batake parade ground (now the training ground for the {{ill|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|en|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|WD=Q889751|lt=Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|1=Japan Ground Self-Defense Force}} {{ill|Yamaguchi Garrison|ja|山口駐屯地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Garrison|1=Camp Yamaguchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467540}}) adjacent to the current location. In 1939 (Showa 14), it was decided to establish a Gokoku Shrine in Yamaguchi Prefecture. The shrine was completed at its current location in August 1941 (Showa 16). It was designated as an Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine, and on November 26 of the same year, 7159 spirits from Yamaguchi Prefecture who were enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine|1=Yasukuni Shrine}} were transferred here. The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : {{ill|Yamaguchi Station|en|Yamaguchi Station|WD=Q861526|lt=Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|1=Yamaguchi Station}} ({{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) {{ill|Yamaguchi Line|en|Yamaguchi Line|WD=Q851226|lt=Yamaguchi Line|1=Yamaguchi Line}}) - 10 minutes by car == Surroundings == * {{ill|Ima Hachiman Shrine|ja|今八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ima Hachiman Shrine|1=Ima-hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65248550}} * {{ill|Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|ja|常栄寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|1=Q11481411|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481411}} * {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|qq=}} * {{ill|Saikotei|ja|山口市菜香亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikotei|1=Q18458034|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18458034}} * {{ill|Kumotani-an|ja|雲谷庵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumotani-an|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ruins * {{ill|Rurikō-ji|en|Rurikō-ji|WD=Q11573988|lt=Rurikō-ji|1=Rurikō-ji}} * {{ill|Dōshun-ji|ja|洞春寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōshun-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nomura Art Museum == References == {{Reflist}} '''Notes''' {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} '''Citations''' {{Reflist}} '''References''' == See also == * {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto|1=State Shinto}} * {{ill|War memorial|ja|戦争祈念施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/War memorial|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=戦争記念施設|1=War memorial|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q575759}} * {{ill|Self-Defense Forces Gokoku Shrine Enshrinement Incident|ja|自衛官護国神社合祀事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Self-Defense Forces Gokoku Shrine Enshrinement Incident|1=Q11613016|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613016}} == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4864182037}} * [http://yamaguchi-gokoku.jp/ Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ }} {{draft categories| [[ja:山口県護国神社]] {{translated page|ja|山口県護国神社|version=102012646|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11467121}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Yamaguchiken gokoku-jinja]][[ja:山口県護国神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Yamaguchiken gokoku-jinja]][[ja:山口県護国神社]] [[ja:山口県護国神社]] {{Cite web|date=2011th year 1月}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine |image=[[File:Yamaguchi_gokoku_shrine.jpg|270px]]
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|marker=place-of-worship}} |location={{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|WD=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}} {{ill|Yamaguchi (city)|en|Yamaguchi (city)|WD=Q207876|lt=Yamaguchi City|1=Yamaguchi (city)}} Hirano 2-2-1 |ISO=JP-35 |latitude degrees=34 |Rank minutes=11 |Rank seconds=37.5 |N/S=N |longitude degrees=131 |longitude minutes=29 |longitude seconds=25.8 |E/W=E |deity=National martyrs related to Yamaguchi Prefecture |Rank={{ill|Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社#指定護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Interior Minister designated Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1534477}}・{{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} |founded=[[1941]] (Showa 16) |main Hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] |festival={{ill|April 29|en|April 29|WD=Q2535|lt=April 29|1=April 29}}・{{ill|November 3|en|November 3|WD=Q2995|lt=November 3|1=November 3}} |map=Japan Yamaguchi#Japan }} '''Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine'''（山口縣護國神社{{ill|Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine|lt=Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|山口縣護國神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=山口県護国神社}}）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}}) located in {{ill|Yamaguchi (city)|en|Yamaguchi (city)|WD=Q207876|lt=Yamaguchi City|1=Yamaguchi (city)}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|WD=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the war dead from Yamaguchi Prefecture who perished in national crises since the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}. This includes notable figures such as {{ill|Yoshida Shōin|en|Yoshida Shōin|WD=Q559747|lt=Yoshida Shoin|1=Yoshida Shōin}}, {{ill|Kusaka Genzui|en|Kusaka Genzui|WD=Q5360999|lt=Kusaka Genzui|1=Kusaka Genzui}}, {{ill|Kijima Matabē|en|Kijima Matabē|WD=Q6406161|lt=Kijima Matabei|1=Kijima Matabē}}, {{ill|Ōmura Masujirō|en|Ōmura Masujirō|WD=Q385551|lt=Omura Masujiro|1=Ōmura Masujirō}}, {{ill|Takasugi Shinsaku|en|Takasugi Shinsaku|WD=Q700310|lt=Takasugi Shinsaku|1=Takasugi Shinsaku}}, and {{ill|Gessho|ja|月性|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gessho|1=Gesshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6605593}}. After {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II|1=World War II}}, Self-Defense Force personnel from Yamaguchi Prefecture who died in the line of duty have also been enshrined. == History == In 1903 (Meiji 36), the {{ill|Bocho Yasukenkai|ja|防長靖献会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bocho Yasukenkai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was established in Yamaguchi Prefecture, and memorial services were held at the Sakura Batake parade ground (now the training ground for the {{ill|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|en|Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|WD=Q889751|lt=Japan Ground Self-Defense Force|1=Japan Ground Self-Defense Force}} {{ill|Yamaguchi Garrison|ja|山口駐屯地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Garrison|1=Camp Yamaguchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467540}}) adjacent to the current location. In 1939 (Showa 14), it was decided to establish a Gokoku Shrine in Yamaguchi Prefecture. The shrine was completed at its current location in August 1941 (Showa 16). It was designated as an Interior Minister-designated Gokoku Shrine, and on November 26 of the same year, 7159 spirits from Yamaguchi Prefecture who were enshrined at {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine|1=Yasukuni Shrine}} were transferred here. The enshrinement ceremony was held on November 27. == location Information == ; location : Yamaguchi Prefecture, Yamaguchi City, Hirano 2-2-1 ; Transportation : Railway : {{ill|Yamaguchi Station|en|Yamaguchi Station|WD=Q861526|lt=Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi Prefecture)|1=Yamaguchi Station}} ({{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) {{ill|Yamaguchi Line|en|Yamaguchi Line|WD=Q851226|lt=Yamaguchi Line|1=Yamaguchi Line}}) - 10 minutes by car == Surroundings == * {{ill|Ima Hachiman Shrine|ja|今八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ima Hachiman Shrine|1=Ima-hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65248550}} * {{ill|Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|ja|常栄寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jouei-ji Temple (Yamaguchi City)|1=Q11481411|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481411}} * {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|qq=}} * {{ill|Saikotei|ja|山口市菜香亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikotei|1=Q18458034|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18458034}} * {{ill|Kumotani-an|ja|雲谷庵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumotani-an|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ruins * {{ill|Rurikō-ji|en|Rurikō-ji|WD=Q11573988|lt=Rurikō-ji|1=Rurikō-ji}} * {{ill|Dōshun-ji|ja|洞春寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōshun-ji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nomura Art Museum == References == {{Reflist}} '''Notes''' {{Reflist|group="Notes"}} '''Citations''' {{Reflist}} '''References''' == See also == * {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|WD=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto|1=State Shinto}} * {{ill|War memorial|ja|戦争祈念施設|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/War memorial|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=戦争記念施設|1=War memorial|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q575759}} * {{ill|Self-Defense Forces Gokoku Shrine Enshrinement Incident|ja|自衛官護国神社合祀事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Self-Defense Forces Gokoku Shrine Enshrinement Incident|1=Q11613016|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11613016}} == External Links == {{Osm box|n|4864182037}} * [http://yamaguchi-gokoku.jp/ Yamaguchi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ }} {{draft categories| [[ja:山口県護国神社]] {{translated page|ja|山口県護国神社|version=102012646|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11467121}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
